method_atom: a clustering/percolation model in which many overlapping extended emitting sources merge into aggregated clusters within a bounded interaction region, so that an initial spatial anisotropy of the source distribution is converted into a directional (azimuthal) anisotropy in the momentum spectrum of the emitted output; the strength and angular shape of the anisotropy depend on the source density, the geometry of the overlap region, and its eccentricity for different overlap fractions.
object_atom: color sources (partons) produced in the early stage of relativistic heavy-ion and proton-proton collisions within the clustering-of-color-sources (string percolation) scheme, characterized by observables such as the elliptic flow v2 versus transverse momentum and the nuclear modification factor versus azimuthal angle across centralities at RHIC and LHC energies.
brief_reason: The overlap-clustering mechanism that turns source-region spatial anisotropy into an azimuthal momentum anisotropy transfers across fields, while the color sources/partons and the v2 and nuclear-modification-factor observables of nuclear collisions are the QCD-specific object.
